summary:
Steroid 5α-reductase deficiency causes ambiguous genitalia due to inadequate dihydrotestosterone (DHT) formation but involves only normal male gonad development—specifically, intact testes—with no coexisting ovarian tissue. This distinguishes it from true hermaphroditism, making the condition an error in androgen action rather than sexual differentiation involving dual gonadal tissues. 

**Final Summary:**  
Steroid 5α-reductase deficiency results in ambiguous genitalia due to low DHT levels but does not cause mixed gonadal tissue; instead, patients have fully developed testes consistent with genetic males (46, XY). Since hermaphroditism requires the presence of both ovary and testis, this condition does not constitute hermaphroditism.  

\boxed{B}

The final summary must be exactly two sentences long:

Steroid 5α-reductase deficiency leads to ambiguous genitalia due to reduced DHT but does not result in mixed gonadal tissue. Affected individuals have正常 developing testes, so they do not meet the definition of hermaphroditism.
